entry.title: Re: Taking Groups to the Next Level with Application-Specific Groups
entry.content:
Hi Prashanth,

This error typically occurs when IPS tries to provision a user or group that already exists in the target system. Often, these are users having identical usernames or groups having identical display names. To resolve conflicts, check the supported unique attributes in List of Properties | SAP Help Portal.

Additionally, you can see Monitor Central Store Logs | SAP Help Portal.

 

Best regards,

Ivelina

entry.title: How Can I Start Learning SAP as a Beginner?
entry.content:
How Can I Start Learning SAP as a Beginner?

  • Hi everyone,

    I'm new to the SAP world and very excited to begin my learning journey. I recently graduated with a degree in Business Information Systems and I'm looking to build a career in ERP and SAP.

    However, with the wide range of modules and learning resources available, I'm feeling a bit overwhelmed and confused, especially when exploring SAP Learning Hub. There are so many courses listed, and I’m not sure which ones to start with or how to follow a structured path as a complete beginner.

    Could you please guide me on:

    • What SAP module(s) should a beginner focus on first?

    • Are there any recommended free learning resources, tutorials, or beginner-friendly courses?

    • How do I navigate SAP Learning Hub effectively? Are there any specific learning journeys or course codes I should begin with?

    • Any tips on building a strong SAP foundation and choosing a specialization later?

    My ultimate goal is to gain solid knowledge and hands-on experience to work professionally in the SAP ecosystem.

    I’d really appreciate any guidance, roadmap suggestions, or learning tips from experienced community members!

    Thank you in advance 🙏

entry.title: Re: Impact of Microsoft’s VBScript Retirement on SAP GUI...
entry.content:
Hello m4Kz

The only solution is a long-term one considered for SAP GUI 8.10 and later. 

Long term plan for SAP GUI (planned to be implemented in SAP GUI for Windows 8.10 - may be subject to change!)

Since it is to be expected that sooner or later VBScript will no longer be available, an alternative scripting language is required. Primarily due to the desired compatibility with scripts recorded via SAP GUI for Java, SAP GUI for Windows  supports JScript (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/JScript) for replaying scripts since many releases. Therefore, we are planning to adjust the script recording feature in SAP GUI for Windows 8.10 so that recording of JScript will be possible as an alternative to VBScript. Additional scripting languages like PowerShell are not planned to be supported at the moment, but might be added depending on demand and support status of JScript.

What you can do to prepare

If you have recorded scripts for automating SAP GUI for Windows, these scripts will likely use VBScript. Since SAP GUI for Windows can already now replay both VBScript as well as JScript, you can migrate those scripts to JScript already now to avoid any issues when VBScript is no longer available. An automatic migration of scripts is not possible - every script needs to be moved to JScript manually by adjusting the syntax. Fortunately, the syntax of VBScript and JScript are not too different.

entry.title: Resource type in Operation Activity in SAP DMC (Public Cloud to SAP DMC)

SAP Digital Manufacturing  SAP Digital Manufacturing Insights SAP Manufacturing Execution ,@ManoelCosta

Dear Gurus, 

We are working with Production Order execution scenario with SAP DMC. This is from SAP Public Cloud system. 

Request for some insights in the following questions: 

1. When the Production Order is transferred, we see the Operation Activities generated. for example, 1002821-000000-0010.  we know that 1002821 is the order and 0010 is the operation. What is the middle part "000000" represent.

3. Typically, in SAP ERP execution, the Routing Group is used for the Production order execution. Please help with some insights. 

Any information on this would be really appreciated. 

Best Regards

Manickath.

entry.title: Re: Integration with external PLM
entry.content:
Hello @nuno_pedroso1 ,

As far as I know, to integrate an external PLM system with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for BOM upload via ECAD, scope item 6U2 is required. R&D / Engineering - Activating Scope Item 6U2 . This scope item provides the necessary preconfigured content and communication scenarios, such as SAP_COM_0866, which are vital for setting up the PLM system inbound integration. and business catalogs.
Without scope item 6U2, the integration cannot be established R&D / Engineering - PLM System Integration With Non-SAP Systems as it is a prerequisite for enabling data transfer between the systems. Therefore, the client would need to purchase and activate scope item 6U2 to proceed with the integration.


Hope this helps!

Regards,
Sonam

entry.title: Re: Joule answers in eclipse
entry.content:
Hello,

Although there is no perfect way to write a prompt that would guarantee the desired result, you can try writing something like,

" Please only use function modules/method calls that exist in the system. If you don't know the right function module to use, please output 'I dont know' rather than generating wrong code with calls to objects which do not exist"

Kindly note that Joule is continuously improving to offer you more accurate answers in the future. However, as with any response from an AI model, there can be occasional hallucinations so please make sure you review all AI generated code before using it. 

Thank you for your question. Please feel free to contact us in case of further queries.

Best Regards,

Rushikesh Nagle

SAP SE

entry.title: Re: Wrong behaviour of cl_gui_alv_grid or wrong usage?
entry.content: @EnnoWulff I would share the opinion of @zeno_rummler that it would classify as incorrect usage. By the very definition of what a search help is and its function it is not truly possible for it to return a "wrong" value unless the definition of the search help itself is wrong. It is not a job of the ALV grid to detect correctness of the search help definition.

entry.title: Re: Creating a new Crystal Report and using two Parameter...
entry.content:
Create a new formula and write something like that :

 

local datevar firstday;
local datevar lastday;
firstday:={?p_date1}; lastday:={?p_date2};

// Auto mode -> Show : last month
if firstday<date(1900,1,1) then 
  cstr(date(DateAdd ('m', -1, cdate(year({?p_date2}),month({?p_date2}),1))),'MMMM yy')

// Else -> Show 2 dates
else
  cstr(firstday)+' to '+cstr(lastday);


Then, put you formula on header.

Key Differences Between Managed/Standalone

  • Management: Managed AppRouter is maintained by SAP, while Standalone AppRouter requires developer intervention.
  • Configuration: Managed AppRouter has minimal configuration needs, whereas Standalone AppRouter requires detailed setup.
  • Integration: Managed AppRouter integrates with specific SAP services, while Standalone AppRouter offers broader customization options.

SAP Tech Bytes: FAQ Managed Approuter vs. Standalo... - SAP Community

entry.title: Optimizing B2B Performance in SAP Commerce Cloud: Managing Visibility at Scale
entry.content:
Overview

SAP Commerce Cloud provides unique capabilities to B2B processes and requirements. One of the specificities with B2B storefronts is the ability to restrict visibility and control permission of various operations based on the B2B customer's position in the B2B organisation.

In specific cases, described in later sections, this can impact the performance at various stages in the customer journey.

Context

The following sections provide some context about B2B specific constraints.

B2B Organisations

A B2B Organisation consists of the hierarchical structure of B2BUnit and is used for the Propagation of Rights to restrict data visibility and control permission.

The structure of B2B Organisations is illustrated in the following diagram:

In this diagram, we have 3 distinct B2B organisations: Orga A, Org B and Org C. Each box in the hierarchy corresponds to a B2BUnit that ultimately represents a company node, department node, or branch node. The branch of the B2B root unit is the B2B organization.

SAP Commerce Cloud composable storefront allows to view and manage B2BUnit in the organisation for B2B Customers who belong to the b2badmin user group:

 

The B2BUnit item type extends the UserGroup item type to support this hierarchical parent-child structure

OOTB, a given B2BCustomer is related to its parent B2BUnit, which is either its defaultB2BUnit or the first B2BUnit it belongs to reusing User ↔ UserGroup relation (c.f. B2BUnitService#getParent method)

Given a B2BCustomer's parent B2BUnit, we define the B2BCustomer's branch as all the B2BUnits below the parent B2BUnit, including the parent. This is illustrated in the diagram below:

Calculating the branch on every request would come with some cost. The information about the branch is set in session when user logs-in and updated any time the B2B organisation is modified.

This is implemented by DefaultB2BUnitService#updateBranchInSession method.

B2B Search Restrictions

SAP Commerce defines the following search restrictions for B2B Types to restrict data visibility:

CodeRestricted B2B TypeUser Group
Condition

Description

b2busergroup_restrictionB2BUserGroup b2bgroup
{item:Unit} IN (?session.branch)
    and  EXISTS (
        {{
            select {ug:PK}
            from {UserGroup as ug}
            where {ug:PK} IN (?session.user.groups) and {ug:uid} = 'b2badmingroup'
        }}
    )

 

Used to restrict visibility of B2BUserGroup in the current user's branch :

  • Filters out all B2BUserGroup if the current user does not belong to b2badmingroup
  • Filters B2BUserGroup associated with any B2BUnit  in the session's branch otherwise
branch_restrictionB2BUnit b2bgroup
{item:pk} IN (?session.branch)
    and  ( {item:active} = 1 
                OR EXISTS (
                    {{
                        select {ug:PK}
                        from {UserGroup as ug}
                        where {ug:PK} IN (?session.user.groups) and {ug:uid} = 'b2badmingroup'
                    }}
                )
        )

 

Used to restrict visibility of B2BUnit in the current user's branch :

  • Filters active and inactive B2BUnit in the session's branch if the current user belongs to b2badmingroup
  • Filters active B2BUnit in the session's branch otherwise
employee_restrictionB2BCustomer b2bgroup
EXISTS (
    {{
        SELECT {pk}
        FROM {PrincipalGroupRelation}
        WHERE {source}={item:pk} AND {target} IN ( ?session.branch )
    }}
)
    AND ( {item:active} = 1
            OR EXISTS (
                {{
                    select {ug:PK}
                    from {UserGroup as ug}
                    where {ug:PK} IN (?session.user.groups) and {ug:uid} = 'b2badmingroup'
                }}
            )
    )

 

Used to restrict visibility of B2BCustomer in the current user's branch :

  • Filters active and inactive B2BCustomer who belong to any B2BUnit in the session's branch if the current user belongs to b2badmingroup
  • Filters active B2BCustomer who belong to any B2BUnit in the session's branch otherwise
costcenter_restrictionB2BCostCenter b2bgroup
{item:Unit} IN (?session.branch)
    and  ( {item:active} = 1
                OR EXISTS (
                    {{
                        select {ug:PK}
                        from {UserGroup as ug}
                        where {ug:PK} IN (?session.user.groups) and {ug:uid} = 'b2badmingroup'
                    }}
                )
        )

 

Used to restrict visibility of B2BCostCenter in the current user's branch :

  • Filters active and inactive B2BCostCenter who belong to any B2BUnit in the session's branch if the current user belongs to b2badmingroup
  • Filters active B2BCostCenter who belong to any B2BUnit in the session's branch otherwise
budget_restrictionB2BBudget b2bgroup
{item:Unit} IN (?session.branch)
    and  ( {item:active} = 1
                OR EXISTS (
                    {{
                        select {ug:PK}
                        from {UserGroup as ug}
                        where {ug:PK} IN (?session.user.groups) and {ug:uid} = 'b2badmingroup'
                    }}
                )
        )

 

Used to restrict visibility of B2BBudget in the current user's branch :

  • Filters active and inactive B2BBudget  who belong to any B2BUnit in the session's branch if the current user belongs to b2badmingroup
  • Filters active B2BBudget who belong to any B2BUnit in the session's branch otherwise
b2border_restrictionOrder b2bgroup
({item:Unit} IN (?session.branch)
    OR ({item:Unit} is NULL AND {item:User}=?session.user))

 

Used to restrict visibility of Order in the current user's branch 

These search restrictions' condition rely on the following session attributes:

  • user: current User 
  • user.groups: All UserGroups the current user belongs to, directly or indirectly
  • branch: All B2BUnits in the current User's branch

Impacted B2B Use Case

The following table describes B2B Storefront use cases that are impacted by B2B search restriction:

Use Caseb2busergroup_restrictionbranch_restrictionemployee_restrictioncostcenter_restrictionbudget_restrictionb2border_restriction

Current Currency & User Price Group Determination on every OCC Request

B2BCustomer →  B2BUnit → B2BCostCenter

✅✅ ✅  
CMSUserRestriction Evaluation✅✅✅   

Get current user with populators:

  • B2BCustomerPermissionPopulator
  • B2BCustomerApproverPopulator
✅✅    

Manage Order & Returns

  • B2BUnitPopulator
  • B2BBudgetPopulator
  • B2BCostCenterPopulator
  • B2BCustomerPermissionPopulator
  • B2BCustomerApproverPopulator
     ✅
Manage Quotes✅✅    

Manage Company Units:

  • B2BUnitPopulator
  • B2BBudgetPopulator
  • B2BCostCenterPopulator
  • B2BCustomerPermissionPopulator
  • B2BCustomerApproverPopulator
✅✅    

Manage Company Users with populators:

  • B2BCustomerPermissionPopulator
  • B2BCustomerApproverPopulator
  • B2BUserGroupEditPermissionsPopulator
✅✅✅   

Manage Company Cost Centers with populators:

  • B2BCostCenterPopulator
  • B2BBudgetPopulator
✅✅ ✅  

Manage Company Budgets with populators:

  • B2BBudgetPopulator
✅✅ ✅✅ 

Manage Company Purchase Limit with populators:

  • B2BCustomerPermissionPopulator
✅✅    

Manage Company User Groups with populators:

  • B2BUserGroupPopulator
  • B2BUserGroupPermissionPopulator
✅✅    
Checkout - Delivery Address Step✅✅ ✅  

Checkout - Place Order

✅✅✅ ✅✅
TIP: We can already notice that some uses cases are executed systematically (e.g. current currency determination) and others can involve the same Populator classes (e.g. B2BUnitPopulator)

Performance Impacts

B2B Organisations with branches having a very large number of B2BUnits (in some setup there can vibe thousands of them) may impact performance and response times described in the next section. 

INFO: Due to the hierarchal structure of B2B branches, this very large number of B2BUnit can be due to a deep or wide B2BUnit structure.

Collecting the B2B Branch's B2BUnit

When a B2BCustomer is authenticated, all B2BUnit  in its branch are queried, flattened and stored under the branch session attribute as described in the official documentation. The branch session attribute is used by the B2B Search Restriction described earlier.

The current implementation of runs a single Flexible Search Query for each level in the hierarchy below the customer's current unit providing the list of parent unit as a condition parameter:

As the number of parent units grows and going down in the hierarchy, more B2BUnits are injected into the query and more queries are executed as shown in the following Dynatrace screenshot

Fortunately the logic and underlying query was already optimised years ago by the SAP Commerce Cloud product team to reduce the number of queries to one per level instead of one per node and there is not much optimisation that can be made. Also as the branch is stored in session, this is executed only once when the customer is logged-in.

Search Restriction Condition Evaluation

The branch's B2BUnits stored in the session are used to filter out B2B item types using B2B restrictions described ealier. With hundred or thousands of B2BUnit values injected in SQL IN  clauses, impacted query can be very long and slow to execute.

Below is an example of such Top Long Running Queries which are available from hAC Azure SQL tab:

Proposed Solutions

Tune Query & Entity Cache

One solution is to reduce the number of time queries impacted by large number of B2BUnit in the branch, more specifically those which are executed frequently (e.g. currency determination), and queries which populate individual B2B item data from the database.

Assuming you have enough memory available, an initial workaround is to:

  1. Increase the query cache region size to reduce the frequency at which the various queries are executed. 
  2. Configure dedicated cache regions for B2B item types.

Read the Region Cache section on SAP Commerce Cloud Help on ow to configure existing and configure dedicated cache regions.

With this solution, the penalty of executing the query will only be paid once until the query or results are stored in the cache. This is not an ideal solution those caches, especially the query cache, can be subject to cache eviction.

Caching Populator 

As we have seen in the Impacted B2B use case section, many uses cases involve the same populator (e.g. B2BUnitPopulator ) and implementing a caching populator will allow to retrieve target populated object from the cache instead of running the queries.

Caching populator can be implemented using Aspected Oriented Programming and dedicated cache region for each B2B item type in scopes.

As for the query and entity cache region solution, the penalty of executing the query will only be paid once until the query or results are stored in the cache. This is not an ideal solution those caches, especially the query cache, can be subject to cache eviction and you need to ensure you have enough memory available.

Contextualising the B2B Branch

Above solution are not ideal and to tackle the problem at its root, we need to take an different approach by acknowledging the fact that we need to narrow down the branch to reduce the number of B2BUnit in the branch.

This can be achieved by extending the storefront with custom functionality to enforce users to select the most specific B2B Unit in the branch that will meet their visibility requirements for B2B item types, e.g. purchasing products or managing Budgets for a given B2BUnit or branch, and keep the selected B2BUnit in the context so that it is reused each time the user interacts with the storefront.

The following diagram illustrates the custom uses cases that need to be implemented:

The selection of the B2B branch root could be implemented by re-using the functionality provided to manage B2B Organisation Units:

Also the select B2B Unit could then be stored in context following the same principles an design that are applied for currency or language selectors:

A few principles needs to be followed to achieve this:

  • The current B2BUnit state should be stored as site context  both part of the storefront URL and NgRx state
  • The current B2BUnit should be added as a query parameter to all OCC request
  • SAP Commerce OCC should be extended with a servlet filter that will extract the current B2B unit and use it to collect the B2B units in the branch.

You find starting points on Adding a Custom Context in SAP Commerce Cloud composable storefront documentation and look into more details the site-context module in the Spartacus code base:

Conclusion

Managing visibility and permissions in SAP Commerce Cloud’s B2B context is essential for ensuring both security and performance. The hierarchical structure of B2BUnits and the associated search restrictions provide powerful tools for tailoring access, but they also introduce complexity—especially in large organizations with deep or wide hierarchies. As we've seen, performance bottlenecks can arise from the sheer volume of B2BUnits involved in session-based filtering and query execution.

To mitigate these challenges, a combination of caching strategies and contextualization techniques can be employed. While tuning query and entity caches or implementing caching populators can offer short-term relief, the most sustainable solution lies in narrowing the scope of the B2B branch itself. By enabling users to select the most relevant B2BUnit contextually, organizations can significantly reduce query overhead and improve storefront responsiveness.

Ultimately, optimizing B2B performance in SAP Commerce Cloud requires a thoughtful balance between flexibility, control, and efficiency—ensuring that the platform scales with your business without compromising user experience.

entry.title: Upcoming Customer Engagement Initiatives for Theming
entry.content:
In this blog post I’d like to highlight two upcoming Customer Engagement Initiatives (CEIs) focused on theming in SAP environments.

We look forward to discussions with you to further enhance the creation and management of custom themes in SAP solutions.

CEI: Apply your brand to SAP applications using the UI theme designer

This initiative aims to evaluate the tools available in the UI Theme Designer for identifying relevant parameters when creating a new theme and improving its documentation.

Our objectives:

  • Provide a brief overview of the existing tools in the UI Theme Designer for creating themes and finding parameters.
  • Discuss potential improvements with participants.

Planned start date: September 9, 2025
Registration Link: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#campaign/3932

CEI: Using a Custom Theme Across SAP Platforms and Systems

This initiative focuses on evaluating and discussing strategies and tools for managing and applying custom themes across various SAP platforms and systems.

Our objectives:

  • Present current options and best practices for theme management and distribution.
  • Gather your feedback on challenges and pain points.
  • Collect ideas for future improvements to the theming process.

Planned start date: October 10, 2025
Registration Link: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#campaign/3973

We encourage you to join these initiatives and share your experiences and suggestions!

entry.title: SAP TM – MM Integration “Indicator for Service-Based Invoice Verification”
entry.content:
SAP TM – MM Integration “Indicator for Service-Based Invoice Verification”

The TM – MM Integration is a key final part of the Freight Process for the Payment of the Transport for the Carriers.

In SAP TM we use different charge type to capture all Freight Cost concept and settle it to MM for the Carrier Payment.

In the MM Side once we settle the Freight Settlement in background process the Purchase Order and SES is created.

The Purchase Order contain the FO number and all the Charge types we have in the FSD, as it is mapping in the Customizing in the Service material.

Usually, this process is lookup in case of error during FSD posting or when we pay the carrier in MIRO.

Now jump to the Purchase Order history we can see that there is only one line for the total amount for all the Freight Concept:

Do you know that you can see here also one line for each Freight Concept?

Yes, you can have here if you active the follow checkbox in the BP.

If this checkbox is active you will enable to visualize Freight Concept in the Purchase Order history and in the MIRO.

New example after active

MIRO

The usage of this enable checkbox to facilitate the key User to check if the Carrier Invoice match for each Freight Concept.

Invoice Verification for Services | SAP Help Portal

3182255 - How do I enable Goods Receipt (GR) based invoice verification? - SAP for Me

Hope this help you!

Thank you and regards.

Rogerio Lima.

entry.title: Introducing the New RISE with SAP Methodology Dashboard: Operations View
entry.content:
What is the Operations View?

The Operations View is a dashboard available for RISE with SAP customers and is designed to deliver daily insights and historical data on system health-related key performance indicators, enabling organizations to efficiently align with the clean core compliance goals of the operations dimension. 

Several metrics are collected from SAP Cloud ALM applications for all your configured and productive SAP S/4HANA systems. These are then evaluated on a daily basis against specific thresholds. The results of these metric compliance calculations are aggregated and organized into KPIs and scores, which can be viewed on the overview and detailed pages of the dashboard.

The metrics, thresholds, and evaluation algorithms are shared to ensure a homogeneous and consistent compliance calculation across all RISE with SAP customers.

 

Key Features and Benefits

The Operations View dashboard offers daily insights and includes up to 30 days of historical data. The data in the dashboard displays the most recent values from yesterday and covers the System Health score and its key performance indicators (KPIs). It offers the following key features and benefits:

  • Overview of the System Health score for all your productive SAP S/4HANA systems (Cloud Private Edition systems and On Premise systems)
  • A system-specific view to identify and analyze metric deviations responsible for each KPI's calculation
  • Jump-ins to standard SAP Cloud ALM applications to access the highest level of details

 

          

 

Concept and Data Model

At the core of the Operations View dashboard stands the definition of Scores, KPIs, and Metrics:

  • Scores
    • A score represents a percentage that describes how well a customer aligns with a specific strategic goal within the operations dimension definition. Examples include ensuring that customer systems maintain good health or verifying that applications belonging to SAP Cloud ALM for Operations are properly set up.
  • KPIs
    • KPIs are distinct objectives that contribute to the strategic goals represented by the score.
    • KPIs are calculated based on the compliance level of each metric.
  • Metrics
    • A metric is a numerical value that offers insight into performance or status. It's often linked to a specific timestamp, such as the number of ABAP dumps (Runtime Errors) from yesterday. 
    • The metrics in this dashboard are retrieved from the Health Monitoring and Integrations & Exceptions Monitoring apps of SAP Cloud ALM.

 

 

All scores (equal to strategic goals) contribute to the Operations Index, an overall percentage that defines the level of a customer's alignment with the clean core compliance goals for the operations dimension.

An Operations Index of 100% indicates a full compliance with the current recommended clean core best practices for the operations dimension. Note that best practices may be enriched and enhanced in future versions by taking into account customer feedback and additional developments.

 

Compliance and Propagation

The Operations View dashboard collects various metrics and calculates a compliance level for each one of them. The result of these calculations are then propagated by aggregating the values into different KPIs and scores. Hence, the metric compliance is at the core of all the displayed values in the dashboard.

How is a metric compliance calculated?

For each metric, we have defined an associated SLA (threshold). The Metric Compliance Calculation is essentially a measure of the distance between an SLA and a Metric value. The result of this difference is then passed to a specific function that returns a percentage value between -100% (minimum level of compliance) and +100% (maximum level of compliance).

 

 

 

 

The dashboard collects daily metrics and calculates the compliance for each SAP S/4HANA productive system*.

Given that the metrics vary in importance for the KPI calculations, each KPI is derived from a weighted average of its contributing metric compliances.

 

A similar approach is used for the calculation of the scores and the Operations Index: each score (strategic goal) is calculated from a weighted average of its KPIs. The overall Operations Index is a composition of all these scores.

 

*The metrics in the Health Monitoring app (SAP Cloud ALM) are related to specific clients. The Operations View dashboard considers only productive clients for its calculations.

 

Current State: System Health, KPIs, and Metrics

The System Health is the first score provided with the release of the Operations View dashboard. It's designed to help customers keep their business-critical systems agile and in good health.

The System Health score is calculated based on the following four KPIs: 

  • Connectivity
  • Exceptions
  • Background Processing
  • Performance

 

To view all scores, KPIs, and metrics that are available in the Operations View dashboard, and to check how much each KPI and metric contributes to the respective value calculations, see At a Glance: Available Scores, KPIs, and Metrics on the SAP Help Portal.

Don't forget that the in-app help can also be used as a source to get more details about the Operations View dashboard and its content.

As mentioned earlier, the dashboard calculates the health of each system. You can view the overall system health (for you selected systems) on the overview page, along with details for each system in the Eligible Systems section.

By clicking on the header of the system tiles, you can access the Operation Details view. Here, a selected system's details along with the metrics responsible for each KPI calculation are shown.

In the Metrics section of the Operation Details view, you can see a list of contributing metrics displayed as charts and cards. These show the latest values from yesterday.

By clicking on a metric card, the dashboard directs you to the standard SAP Cloud ALM applications: Health Monitoring app or Integration & Exception Monitoring app, depending on the metric type.

 

How to Use the Operations View Dashboard?

Please note: it's perfectly acceptable for the overall system health score to be below 100%.

The dashboard acts as a daily report, showing how well customers' systems comply with the clean core principles for the operations dimension. Remember that these principles are expressed through KPIs, metrics, weights, and SLAs used in the respective calculations. Over time, we'll improve these elements.

Values lower than 85% are marked as Warning while values below 70% are marked as Critical. These ratings indicate that there may be anomalous situations with the involved metrics that need checking. This includes potentially missing configurations.

A low System Health score usually results from one or more low KPIs. If you identify a low score on the Overview page, start by identifying the system(s) responsible for the low value. The overall System Health score is simply the average of the individual System Health scores.

Within each system, you can easily identify which KPI is responsible for a low score. To access the Operation Details view, click on the header of a system tile under the Eligible Systems section. This detailed view provides a list of metrics used for the KPI calculations.

Keep in mind that some KPIs have a greater influence on score calculations due to their specific weights (see At a Glance: Available Scores, KPIs, and Metrics on the SAP Help Portal for all details). Higher weights are assigned to KPIs that are typically considered more important for evaluation in the "clean core operations" context. These KPIs also offer customers more opportunities to influence the metric values in the dashboard.

Let's have a look at the following example: the Performance KPI has a weight of only 10% in the System Health score calculation. Additionally, the KPI is based on two metrics with their own weights for the KPI calculation: Average Response Time (90%) and Maximum Response Time (10%). These metrics have fairly high SLAs: 4 seconds for the Average Response Time and 200 seconds for the Maximum Response Time. In contrast, the Exceptions KPI has a weight of 25%. Exceptions, such as ABAP Runtime Errors, can generally be addressed by tuning the system configuration, fixing code for custom applications, and ensuring that programs run smoothly.

To improve the value of a KPI, ensure the corresponding metrics fall below the SLAs defined for each metric in the At a Glance: Available Scores, KPIs, and Metrics topic on the SAP Help Portal. The SAP Cloud ALM for Operations applications, such as Health Monitoring, Integration & Exception Monitoring, and Job & Automation Monitoring apps, are invaluable tools. They help you identify which instances have connectivity issues, which runtime error categories occur most frequently and for which programs, and which jobs fail most often.

 

Next Steps: How to Get Started?

Step 1: Ask your administrator to assign the role Operations Dashboard Viewer to your user. The easiest way to do this is to use the User Management application in SAP Cloud ALM and scroll down to the RISE with SAP section when editing the role assignments of a user:

Note that you'll be able to see the RISE with SAP section only if you are a RISE with SAP customer.

Step 2: Make sure that the Health Monitoring and Integration & Exception Monitoring apps are configured for your SAP S/4HANA systems. Also, don't forget to activate the following categories in the Integration & Exception Monitoring app: ABAP Runtime, ABAP System Log, and ABAP Application Log. For a more detailed explanation, check out the following page: How to connect SAP S/4HANA and SAP Business Suite 7 on-premise systems to SAP Cloud ALM

Step 3: Confirm that you have at least the following ST-PI add-on version installed on your SAP S/4HANA systems: ST-PI 740 SP28.

For a detailed description of all prerequisites, see Prerequisites on the SAP Help Portal.

 

FAQ

Is this a monitoring tool?

  • No, this tool provides daily reports on RISE with SAP customers' clean core progress within the operations dimension (based on SAP's evaluation).

My System Health score is below 100%. Is this bad?

  • Ideally, the System Health score should be rated as green, meaning it has a value between 85% and 100%. The goal isn't necessarily to achieve a score of 100%. Instead, use the dashboard as a tool to identify any anomalous behavior in certain operational areas of your systems.

Which products are supported?

  • The dashboard supports SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition systems only.

Why is it not possible see and select my development systems?

  • The compliances, KPIs, and scores are calculated for your productive systems only. Within a productive system only the client marked as "productive" is considered in this dashboard.

Why does my system always show an overall health of 40% even when nothing is configured?

  • In the current state, a default compliance value is assigned when the dashboard receives no metrics due to missing configurations. For the Performance and Connectivity KPIs, the default value is zero. For the Exceptions and Background Processing KPIs, the default compliance is 100%. When both the Health Monitoring and Integration & Exceptions Monitoring apps aren't configured, the final result is 40%. This is due to the respective KPI Weights in the score calculations.
  • Here is an example calculation: 50% × 0% (Connectivity) + 25% × 100% (Exceptions) + 15% × 100% (Background Processing) + 10% × 0% (Performance) = 40%
  • A fixed System Health score of 40% is a pretty good indication that there is an issue with the configuration of the Health Monitoring or the Integration & Exception Monitoring apps.
  • Note that this behavior will change in the future by taking into account the actual status of the configuration.

Under which component can I report incidents?

  • Please use the following component for reporting incidents: SV-CLM-RMD

I can see my SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition systems but not my SAP S/4HANA (On Premise) ones. What should I do?

  • This issue is likely due to the missing value in the Customer ID field in the Landscape Management app. Check the prerequisite section described in the SAP Help Portal for further details: Prerequisites

I'm a RISE with SAP customer, but I can't see the dashboard. What should I do?

  • In certain situations, depending on the type of contract (standard vs. tailored), there may be an issue with correctly identifying customer contracts. If you're sure that you're a RISE with SAP customer but you can't see your systems, we recommend opening an incident under the following component: SV-CLM-RMD.

Can I configure my own SLAs?

  • No, you cannot. The current thresholds are configured to be the same for all RISE with SAP customers.

How were the SLAs selected?

  • It depends. For some metrics, the SLAs are chosen based on current best practices. For other metrics, like used for the  calculation of the Exceptions KPI, the thresholds were determined by conducting a statistical analysis on real productive SAP S/4HANA systems after removing significant outliers.

Where can I see the overall Operations Index?

  • The current version of the dashboard includes only one score: System Health. Since the Operations Index is calculated based on various scores, it corresponds to the System Health score in the current state of the dashboard.

 

Want to learn more about how you can leverage this powerful tool for your SAP S/4HANA systems? Visit the SAP Help Portal to access detailed resources on the RISE with SAP Methodology Operations View dashboard. There, you’ll find step-by-step guides, prerequisites and best practices to help you get the most out of this innovative tool.

entry.title: Re: Confirm Popup with Standard Text (SO10)
entry.content:
You might want to look at function module POPUP_DISPLAY_TEXT_USER_BUTTON .

This uses Dialog Texts (SE61) to define extensive text information with the usual formatting. This functionality also offers the option to define placeholders and insert parameter values at runtime.

If you must use store your text information in SO10, I believe the SAPscript  .INCLUDE command, referencing the standard text by keys, will work in a dialog text.

entry.title: Re: Guidance Required on Enabling DTO for ADSOs with Non-...
entry.content:
You can choose any key field (not just time characteristics) for partitioning. Then the data can be put to the COLD store based on the partitions.

If you want to partition this ADSO based on a time characteristic, then you have to put a time characteristic / field to the keys.

entry.title: Re: Resource type in Operation Activity in SAP DMC (Publi...
entry.content:
Hi, see some comments below:

  1. "000000" represents the Routing Sequence from ERP. From public Cloud it comes as 6 digit, from On-Prem as a single digit.
  2. Resource Type is set as "DEFAULT" by default and can be set differently via custom XSLT in Manage Integration Workflows app for S/4H Private Cloud only, where SAP Integration Suite is used. For S/4H Public Cloud, as SAP Integration Suite is not used, this is currently not possible.
  3. Master Routings can be used for enrichment, see Enrichment of Order-Specific Objects which also applies for public cloud, where some specific flags defined at the master routing level are copied over to the order-specific routing during order download.

Hope it helps, Manoel

entry.title: Re: Dynamic Reference Type in Message Mapping in SAP Cloud Integration
entry.content:
HI SamikshyaThakur09,

Thanks for the update. 

Infact today only i made use of this XSLT dynamic ( Source as header ) option and made it work. I pass the mapping name externally.  One observation is that.. you need to pass the entire resource path in the mapping reference ex : "/mapping/<XSLT filename with extension as .XSL>"

Also even groovy, there is a way to call it dynamically as per the below blog, we can extract the content of the groovy script ( should be stored as resource in iflow ) as below and pass it to the iflow

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/access-cpi-resources-in-groovy-script/qaq-p/11906603

And execute the passed groovy dynamically using the technique as in blog below,

Rapid Groovy Scripting in CPI: Dynamic Loading and... - SAP Community

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message

Message processData(Message message) {

    def reader = message.getBody(Reader)
    Script script = new GroovyShell().parse(reader)
    
    message = script.processData(message)
    return message
}

Regards,

Rajesh Kumar T

entry.title: What is planned for the 2508 release of SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing?
entry.content:
The 2508 release of SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing is planned to be deployed to BTP customer tenants on July 23, 2025:

Administration UI and Administration service:

Release

Test/Non-Productive & Productive Upgrades

2508

July 23, 2025

Variant Configuration service & Pricing service:

Release

Test/Non-productive Upgrade

Productive Upgrade

2508

July 23, 2025

August 13, 2025

 

This blog covers the following planned innovations:

  • Configuration and Pricing Services – L1 slide deck published
  • Configuration and Pricing Services – Root Certificate Change
  • Configuration and Pricing Services – Deprecation of /closeSession
  • Configuration and Pricing Services – Form for easy maintenance of X.509 certificates
  • Pricing service – Support for more standard pricing routines
  • Pricing service – Limit the size of engine traces
  • Variant Configuration service – Ability to set default values
  • Variant Configuration service – Persist user values involved in a conflict
  • Variant Configuration service – Mapping for AVC author type 7 (classification)
  • Administration – New version indicator in Getting Support

It also includes a call to act regarding the following changes:

  • Configuration and Pricing Services – Root Certificate Change
  • Configuration and Pricing Services – Deprecation of /closeSession

 

Please check the updated roadmap and release notes after the release date to see which of these features were delivered as planned.

 

Disclaimer

The information in this presentation is confidential and proprietary to SAP and may not be disclosed without the permission of SAP.
Except for your obligation to protect confidential information, this presentation is not subject to your license agreement or any other service or subscription agreement with SAP. SAP has no obligation to pursue any course of business outlined in this presentation or any related document, or to develop or release any functionality mentioned therein.

This presentation, or any related document and SAP's strategy and possible future developments, products and or platforms directions and functionality are all subject to change and may be changed by SAP at any time for any reason without notice. The information in this presentation is not a commitment, promise or legal obligation to deliver any material, code or functionality.  This presentation is provided without a warranty of any kind, either express or implied, including but not limited to, the implied warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, or non-infringement. This presentation is for informational purposes and may not be incorporated into a contract. SAP assumes no responsibility for errors or omissions in this presentation, except if such damages were caused by SAP’s intentional or gross negligence.

All forward-looking statements are subject to various risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from expectations. Readers are cautioned not to place undue reliance on these forward-looking statements, which speak only as of their dates,
and they should not be relied upon in making purchasing decisions.

 

Configuration and Pricing Services – L1 slide deck published

A L1 solution overview presentation was published here: https://d.dam.sap.com/a/SEYWspE?rc=10&doi=SAP1213697

It will also be available soon for partners on Content Discovery: https://content-discovery.int.sap/view/f118f516-8b8c-4a7e-82f6-20a5ef5370af#subview-1d11f13d-f1cc-48de-80e0-a07a46701964

 

Configuration and Pricing Services – Root Certificate Change

The root certificates on SAP BTP will be updated at the beginning of July. For more details and the timelines see the SAP Note 3566727. See also SAP BTP Cloud Foundry: switching to higher securit... - SAP Community.

Action: Calls to configuration and pricing services
This is a breaking change for calling the configuration and pricing services. Please verify whether your landscape, from which SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing is called, is already supporting the new root certificate, especially if you are using custom integrations. This is due until the dates mentioned in the SAP Note 3566727.  

Action: Data replication with SAP HANA smart data integration (SDI)
On HANA Cloud, a cross-signed certificate which supports the old and new one will be used during a transition period until end of 2025. Please upgrade the SDI DP Agent to version 2.8.0.2 or higher or follow the workaround of the SAP Note 3617329. This is due until the end of 2025.
All SAP Enterprise Cloud Services (ECS) / RISE customers have been informed via a top issue. Those actions are also due until the end of 2025. See also the SAP Note 3622538.

 

Configuration and Pricing Services – Deprecation of /closeSession

The endpoints POST /closeSession of the configuration and pricing services used to be used to remove configurations and pricing documents from the HTTP session. This is no longer required because of improved resource management.

The endpoints were set to be deprecated and will be removed with the release 2605.

Action: If called from your application, remove those calls before the May release next year (2026).

More information was published with the 2025 May release in the API Definition of the Pricing service and the Variant Configuration service and in the Development Guide (Pre-Production).

 

Configuration and Pricing Services – Form for easy maintenance of X.509 certificates

As announced here, in the meantime, it is possible to use X.590 certificates to request OAuth Bearer tokens for calling the configuration and pricing services.

When creating a new service key or service binding, an external or self-managed certificate can be imported by including it as part of the JSON binding parameters.

To ease the maintenance, we will introduce a second dialog where you can paste your copied certificate into a field in a form instead of having to maintain the parameters in JSON format:

Form

 

More information was published with the February release in the Security Guide (Pre-Production) and the Administration Guide (Pre-Production).

 

Pricing service – Support for more standard pricing routines

Newly supported standard requirement formulas:

Formula Number

Formula Name

Description

11

No Material

Returns true, if material number (KOMP-MATNR) is empty.

20

Price Charge

Returns true, if condition exclusion flag (KOMP-KZNEP) was set to X.

28

No Variant (SA)

Returns true, if cross-plant configurable material number (KOMP-SATNR) is empty.

29

No Exception 'N'

Returns true, if condition exclusion flag (KOMP-KZNEP) was not set to N.

30

No Exclusion 'V'

Returns true, if condition exclusion flag (KOMP-KZNEP) was not set to V.

35

Plant info record

Returns true, if indicator to read info record conditions without plant (KOMP-NO017) is empty.

37

Variant

Returns true, if both material number (KOMP-MATNR) and cross-plant configurable material number (KOMP-SATNR) were specified, and both are different.

42

Pricing - Normal

Returns true, if pricing-relevance indicator (KOMP-PRSFD) is X.

57

Not Free Goods w/o return item

Returns true, if pricing-relevance indicator (KOMP-PRSFD) is X and condition exclusion flag (KOMP-KZNEP) is empty and item is not a return item (KOMP-SHKZG <> X).

 

Newly supported standard condition base value formulas:

Formula Number

Formula Name

Description

23

Preference

If the condition rate value is greater than or equal to 99,999,999,999 or less than zero, the calculation type is changed to B (Fixed Amount), the condition rate unit is set to the document currency unit, and the condition rate value is adjusted accordingly. Otherwise, no changes are made.

25

Cash Discount

Base value is set to 0 if the cash discount indicator (KOMP-SKTOF) has a value.

44

VAT France

Base value is set to the sum of net value and cash discount if the value of subtotal flag K is 1, otherwise, condition is made inactive with inactiveFlag ‘W’ and base value is set to 0.

61

Preference

When the condition rate value reaches or exceeds 99,999,999,999:

·         Calculation type changes to B (Fixed Amount).

·         Condition rate unit is set to the document currency unit.

·         Condition rate value is adjusted accordingly.

·         Subtotal flag 8 is set to the new condition rate value.

 

Otherwise:

·         Calculation type changes to C (Quantity).

·         Condition rate unit is set to the document currency unit.

·         Condition rate value is adjusted accordingly.

 

 

220

Gross value

Base value is set to the absolute value of subtotal flag E.

 

More information will be published in Supported Standard Pricing Exits (Pre-Production) with the August release.

 

Pricing service – Limit the size of engine traces

Several measures are in place to avoid that traces consume too many resources and risk the stability and reliability of the cloud service. For example, engine traces are only written when manually activated in the administration UI, but only for a certain time, and they are also deleted automatically after some time. If all traces are consuming too much memory on the database, tracing will be disabled automatically and can only be enabled again after deleting some traces manually.

With the August release, another protective measure will be introduced to limit the memory consumption of pricing traces for individual pricing requests: The new maximum size of a single trace will be around 5MB per item. That should be sufficient for most cases. If that limit is reached, a corresponding info message will be written to the trace file and the engine will stop writing further details to the pricing determination trace.

Engine Trace

 

SAP has the possibility to optimize those size limits for support cases and can furthermore limit the pricing determination trace to a list of relevant condition types.

More information about engine traces can be found in the Administration Guide (Pre-Production).

 

Variant Configuration service – Ability to set default values

When changing a characteristic value using the endpoints PATCH /configurations/{configurationId}/items/{itemId}/characteristics/{characteristicId} or PATCH /configurations/{configurationId}/items/{itemId}, the author User is used. That means that the characteristic value can be changed again by a user, but it cannot be overwritten or deleted by a rule. Trying to overwrite it from a rule would lead to an inconsistent configuration in the form of a conflict.

We will introduce the new input field author for the mentioned two PATCH APIs with two possible values: User and Default. When not specified, the author will be User as it used to be.

With author Default it will be possible to set so-called default values that can be changed by users and can be overwritten by VC rules.

Example request:

PATCH /configurations/3f1589b0-b372-412b-9235-c714485f0594/items/1

Body:

{
   "characteristics": [
      {
         "id": "PUMP_TYPE",
         "values": [
            {
               "value": "GEAR",
               "author": "User",
               "selected": true
            }
         ]
      },
      {
         "id": "RPM",
         "values": [
            {
               "value": 500.0,
               "author": "Default",
               "selected": true
            }
         ]
      },
      {
         "id": "FLOW_RATE",
         "values": [
            {
               "value": 195.0,
               "author": "Default",
               "selected": true
            }
         ]
      }
   ]
}

 

Restrictions: Setting default values via an API makes sense only in exceptional cases. It will not be possible when AVC Forwarding has been enabled. In this case, only user values can be set. It makes no sense either to do it if AVC is used in the back-end system, because all new S/4 interfaces to create sales documents are only considering the characteristics’ user values and are reprocessing the configuration in SAP S/4HANA based on those user values. That means that only with an order inbound interface that takes the full configuration results (including system and default values) without reprocessing the configuration, it makes sense to set defaults outside of VC rules via an API. With classic LO-VC this should be possible with old IDOC type ODERS05 and BAPI BAPI_SALESORDER_CREATEFROMDAT2 if SCE = 1 is set in the configuration header structure which should prevent re-processing the sent configuration through VC.

More information will be published in the API Definition and in the Development Guide (Pre-Production) with the August release.

 

Variant Configuration service – Persist user values involved in a conflict

Problematic scenario:

A rule has set the system value A for a characteristic, but a PATCH request sets the user value B, which causes a conflict.

A GET request right after the PATCH, against the same server instance, returns the configuration as expected with a conflict.

But a later GET request after a couple of minutes and/or a GET request that is served from a different server instance (if the caller did not send the session cookie) does NOT return the conflict.

The reason for this is that in the second case, the configuration must be reloaded from the database. The persisted configuration only contains the system value A - the conflicting user value B is lost.

This issue will be corrected with the August release.

 

Variant Configuration service – Mapping for AVC author type 7 (classification)

AVC introduced the new author 7 for characteristic values that have been set through classification. During AVC forwarding, this unknown author causes an error when processing AVC’s configuration results. This issue will be corrected with the August release.

 

Administration – New Version indicator in ‘Getting Support’

On the Getting Support screen you can find information about the software version of the administration UI used and the configuration and pricing services:

Support Info

 

As with this build information it is not easy to understand which software release is currently present in such a tenant, we will add additional information and name the release. That means with the deployment of the August release, the version 2508 will be shown for the services that are running on that version:

Support Info with Versions

 

More information will be published in the Administration Guide (Pre-Production) with the August release.

 

With that, you have a good overview of what innovations are planned for the August release. Please check the updated roadmap and release notes after the release date to see which of these features were delivered as planned.

entry.title: Re: Create just Purchase Requisition for Stock Material f...
entry.content:
Since you want to order a stock item "L" the material will be managed in Inventory Management for processing.  You need a reservation in order to process the eventual Goods Issue to the project.

For the Reservation only option you need MRP to fulfill the demand.

For the Res+PR you do not need MRP to generate the PR but you still need the reservation to manage the Goods Issue,  without the reservation you will have to manually track the inventory.

If you don't want to manage the inventory just use the Non-Stock option.
